title: Gonioanthela axillaris
intro: Gonioanthela axillaris, also known as the 'Hairy Gonio', is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Gonioanthela axillaris include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gonioanthela axillaris is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the production of traditional remedies such as teas, tinctures, and capsules.
